name: Mrs Fields' Marbles
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 300°F.
2. In a medium bowl, whisk together flour, baking powder, and salt until well combined.
3. Set aside.
4. In a large bowl, mix sugars with an electric mixer on medium speed until smooth.
5. Add butter and beat until the batter is grainy and well mixed.
6. Add egg, sour cream, and vanilla extract, then beat until light and fluffy.
7. Scrape the bowl clean.
8. Gradually add the flour mixture to the sugar mixture, stirring at low speed until just combined.
9. Place chocolate chips in a double boiler over hot (but not boiling) water, stirring constantly until melted.
10. Alternatively, place chips in a microwave-safe bowl and zap on high, stirring every 20 seconds until melted.
11. Allow the chocolate to cool slightly before pouring it over the cookie batter.
12. Using a wooden spoon or rubber spatula, gently fold the melted chocolate into the dough until just combined.
13. Do not mix the chocolate completely into the dough.
14. Drop rounded tablespoons of dough, about 2 inches apart, onto ungreased cookie sheets.
15. Bake for 23-25 minutes, or until lightly golden brown.
16. Quickly transfer cookies to a cool surface to stop cooking and allow them to set.
